---
title: "Credits & Positioning"
subtitle: "Gracious credit to the leaderboard champions and the public-notebook lineage we learned from"
toc: true
---

This project stands on the shoulders of the ISIC-2024 community. We are explicit about what we
**reused** versus what is **ours**, and we credit the people whose work taught us the task.

## The leaderboard champions {#champions}

We did **not** beat the unconstrained private leaderboard, and we are not trying to — we banned
the two ingredients (external data, synthetic positives) that the top teams relied on. Their
results are the benchmark our *constrained* claim is positioned against, and they deserve full
credit.

::: {.callout-note}
## 1st place — Ilya Novoselskiy
Private pAUC **0.17264** (post-competition reports cite ~0.1755 for the best unconstrained
configuration). An ensemble of **EVA-02 + EdgeNeXt** image models fused with a **GBDT** tabular
stack. Crucially, the solution used **external ISIC-archive dermoscopy data** *and* **~30,000
Stable-Diffusion-synthesized malignant lesions** — both of which we **deliberately ban**.

**The most instructive detail for our study:** their own ablation reports the ~30k synthetic
lesions added only **+0.0007** pAUC. On a 393-positive task, fabricating 30k positives bought
almost nothing — a sobering data point for the "just add synthetic data" reflex, and part of
why we judged the label-validity risk not worth taking.
:::

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
## 2nd & 3rd place
- **2nd — `uchiyama33`** — strong image + tabular ensemble; a source of the EMA + mixup recipe we
  adopted for the small backbone.
- **3rd — `kyohei-123`** — likewise a top image/tabular blend.

Both are credited for the public methodology that shaped the field's image-expert recipe.
:::

## The public-notebook lineage {#lineage}

Our tabular feature engineering and overall recipe descend directly from the public Kaggle
notebooks. We learned the task from them and reuse their ideas with attribution:

| Source | What we learned / reused |
|---|---|
| **greysky** — "only tabular" | The LightGBM hyperparameters and the tabular-first thesis. Our `lgb_bag` params are greysky-lineage; the bagging + undersampling structure follows this work. |
| **snnclsr** | Tabular feature-engineering patterns and CV structure. |
| **motono0223** | Image-baseline training recipe (small backbones, undersampling). |
| **andreasbis** — "CNN preds as features" | The core stacking idea: feed an image model's OOF probability into the GBDT as a feature. |
| **richolson** | Additional baseline ideas and ensembling practice. |

We also vendored the **official ISIC-2024 scorer** (`src/metric_official.py`, © 2024 N. R.
Kurtansky, MSKCC) verbatim and attributed, purely to *prove* our metric matches it.

## What we reused vs. what is ours {#contributions}

::: {.callout-tip}
## Reused (with attribution)
- The **tabular-first thesis** and LightGBM hyperparameter lineage (greysky).
- The **CNN-preds-as-a-feature** stacking idea (andreasbis).
- The **EMA + mixup** small-backbone recipe (2nd/4th-place public methodology).
- The **official pAUC scorer** (Kurtansky / MSKCC), vendored to verify our implementation.
:::

::: {.callout-important}
## Our own contributions
1. **The efficiency frontier as the deliverable.** We reframe the task from "one private-LB
   number" to a **quality-vs-cost Pareto frontier** across params, FLOPs, and CPU latency — so a
   model only counts if it earns its cost. Every reported model is one measured point.
2. **The no-external / no-synthetic discipline, made rigorous.** A locked scope
   (`docs/DECISIONS.md`), a `cv-guardian` with veto power, and a hard no-leak guarantee — turning
   "we didn't cheat" into something *audited*, not asserted.
3. **The image-space ugly-duckling.** Extending the tabular ugly-duckling idea into the image
   expert (a within-patient image-deviation signal) — tried, measured, and reported even though
   it did **not** help, because honest negatives are the point.
4. **A complete, honest negative-results catalogue.** Meta-stacker, learned MoE gate, image
   embeddings, heavy transformers, over-strong EMA — every one logged with its number. The
   finding that *complexity consistently lost at 393 positives* is itself the contribution.
:::

## Where our claim sits {#claim-position}

::: {.callout-warning}
## Honest positioning
- We claim **SOTA within the no-external-data / no-synthetic / single-dataset class**, on a
  transparent efficiency frontier with leak-audited CV.
- We **do not** claim to beat the champions' unconstrained ~0.1755 — that used the very
  ingredients we banned, and matching it is impossible by construction.
- Our best constrained point is **OOF pAUC 0.17430** at 15.8 M params / 2.46 GFLOPs / 61 ms CPU.
  The honest private-test projection is **that minus ~0.01–0.02** (see [Results](results.qmd)).
:::

---

*Continue to [References →](references.qmd)*
